fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 63 uses `>` instead of `>=`, so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly returns `True` (allowed) instead of `False` (blocked).

spec.expr: len(recent__1) < limit or len(recent__1) > limit or (len(recent__1) == limit)

constraints.intent: When len(recent) equals limit the branch should be taken (blocked), so the condition must be >= not >.
After the else branch (not blocked), recent count must be strictly less than limit; equality should have been caught by the if branch.
Capture the case where len(recent) equals limit — the gate should block but the > condition misses this.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and len([t for t in timestamps if t >= now - window]) >= limit)

constraints.intent: The function must never return allowed=True when recent event count meets or exceeds the limit.
